reasoning:
The current baseline value for April 13 is 2726 (which is the observation for April 9, released April 10). The question asks for the probability of future releases exceeding this threshold. 
1. April 20 resolution (April 16 data): April 15 is Tax Day, which typically drains banking liquidity into the Treasury General Account, causing a temporary spike in discount window primary credit (as seen in April 2024's jump). The FRED statistical model also firmly predicts a ~66% chance of exceeding 2726 next week based on current trend and seasonality. Thus, 0.66.
2. May 13 resolution (May 7 data): After the tax deadline squeeze passes, reserve balances will eventually normalize. In March and early April, the series was hovering between 2100 and 2400. Once the tax spike recedes, the value is likely to return below the 2726 threshold. Factoring in increasing uncertainty over a 1-month horizon, the probability is set slightly below 50% at 0.46. 
3. July 12 and Oct 10: For these longer horizons (3-month and 6-month), the overall trajectory of discount window usage is highly uncertain. It may slowly drift up if Quantitative Tightening continues to drain reserves, or remain suppressed if no banking stress materializes. Following the rule to increase uncertainty and pull towards 0.5 over longer horizons, these probabilities are set to 0.48 and 0.50 respectively.
